steps.title: Set up the Pythagorean theorem
steps.content:
For a right triangle with one leg equal to 43.2 mm and hypotenuse equal to 196.8 mm, apply the Pythagorean theorem with the unknown leg $b$:
$$43.2^2 + b^2 = 196.8^2$$

steps.title: Solve for the unknown leg
steps.content:
Compute the squares:
$$1866.24 + b^2 = 38730.24$$
Subtract 1866.24 from both sides:
$$b^2 = 36864$$
Take the positive square root:
$$b = 192$$
This matches choice C — C is correct.
